file.content:
# Review: `deepmind/1070.lean` — Erdős Problem 1070

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line (`import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`), immediately after the copyright block, separated by one blank line.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 1070`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/1070`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (maximal independent set in unit distance graphs, estimate f(n), is f(n) ≥ n/4?).
- [x] Bibliography entries for related papers: [Er87b], [Cr67], [MRVZ23].

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1070 ... end Erdos1070`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos1070`).
- [x] No `open` / `open scoped` statements needed; all names resolve through `ProblemImports`. N/A — pass.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- N/A — no custom `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations in this file.

## 6. Theorem Statements

### Main theorem `erdos_1070` (line 52)

- [x] Uses `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Named `erdos_1070` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5 52]`.
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`) referencing [Er87b].

### `answer()` usage — Case 1 (yes/no question)

The original problem asks: *"Is it true that f(n) ≥ n/4?"* — this is a yes/no question → Case 1.

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P`.
- [x] RHS `P` formalizes the question as originally asked (for every n-point set in ℝ², there exists a subset of ≥ n/4 points with pairwise distance ≠ 1).
- [x] Problem is open, so `answer(sorry)` is correct (not `answer(True)` or `answer(False)`).
- [x] Quantifiers (`∀ (n : ℕ), ...`) go **after** `answer(sorry) ↔`, not as theorem parameters.

### Variant `erdos_1070.variants.lower_bound` (line 66)

- [x] Uses `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Named `erdos_1070.variants.lower_bound` — follows naming convention.
- [x] Has `@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]`.
- [x] Has docstring referencing [Cr67].
- [x] Direct assertion (Case 3) — no `answer()` needed; this is a known result stated as a direct bound.
- [x] Parameters `(n : ℕ) (hn : n ≥ 1) (f : ...)` are appropriate for a universally-quantified direct assertion.

### Variant `erdos_1070.variants.upper_bound` (line 81)

- [x] Uses `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Named `erdos_1070.variants.upper_bound` — follows naming convention.
- [x] Has `@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]`.
- [x] Has docstring referencing the Moser spindle.
- [x] Direct assertion (Case 3) — existential statement about a configuration, no `answer()` needed.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
- [x] Values used: `research open` (main), `research solved` (both variants) — all valid.
- [x] The `research open` theorem (`erdos_1070`) contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed (two: 5, 52).
- [x] Numbers are in **ascending numeric order** (5 < 52).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both 5 (combinatorics) and 52 (convex and discrete geometry) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Main theorem uses `by\n  sorry` (multi-line `sorry` format — acceptable).
- [x] Both variants use `by\n  sorry`.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions (no definitions present).
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] Variants named `erdos_1070.variants.lower_bound` and `erdos_1070.variants.upper_bound`.
- [x] All variants live in the **same file** as the main theorem.
- [x] Each variant has its own `@[category ..., AMS ...]` attributes.
- [x] Both variants have docstrings explaining what they formalize (Croft lower bound, Moser spindle upper bound).

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used (`∀`, `∃`, `∈`, `≥`, `≠`, `↔`, `ℕ`, `ℝ`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
- [ ] Custom linters — not verified in this review.

---

**Verdict: PASS** — all checklist items that can be statically verified are satisfied. The file is clean, well-structured, and follows all style conventions. The `answer()` usage correctly identifies the problem as a yes/no question (Case 1). Variants are properly named, categorized, and documented. Build verification is pending.

content.text: The review verdict is **PASS**. No changes needed.
